name: quantization

members.name: dequantize
members.signature: dequantize(q: Tensor, scale: _ScaleLike, zero_point: _ZeroPointLike, ch_axis: int | None = None)
members.summary: Invert `quantize`: $\hat{x} = (q - z)\,s$ in `float32`.

parameters.name: ch_axis
parameters.description: Channel axis for per-channel; `None` for per-tensor.

returns.description: Reconstructed `float32` approximation of the original tensor.

members.name: fake_quantize
members.signature: fake_quantize(x: Tensor, scale: _ScaleLike, zero_point: _ZeroPointLike, quant_min: int, quant_max: int, ch_axis: int | None = None)
members.summary: Simulate quantization in the float domain with a straight-through grad.
members.extended:
Returns $\hat{x} = \text{dequantize}(\text{quantize}(x))$ as a
`float32` tensor.  The backward pass is the straight-through
estimator — identity where the code lands in `[quant_min,
quant_max]` and zero where it saturates — so `fake_quantize` is
differentiable and usable inside quantization-aware training.

parameters.name: ch_axis
parameters.description: Channel axis for per-channel; `None` for per-tensor.

members.name: quantize
members.signature: quantize(x: Tensor, scale: _ScaleLike, zero_point: _ZeroPointLike, qdtype: QDtype, ch_axis: int | None = None)
members.summary: Map a real tensor to integer codes stored in `qdtype.storage`.
members.extended:
Computes $q = \operatorname{clip}(\operatorname{round}(x/s)+z, q_\min, q_\max)$ in the float domain, then casts to the integer
storage dtype.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Quantization step `s`.  A length-`C` tensor for per-channel.

parameters.name: zero_point
parameters.description: Integer offset `z` mapping real 0 to a code.

parameters.name: ch_axis
parameters.description: Channel axis for per-channel quantization; `None` for per-tensor.

members.name: QParams
members.signature: QParams(scale: Tensor, zero_point: Tensor, qscheme: QScheme, qdtype: QDtype, ch_axis: int | None = None)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(scale: Tensor, zero_point: Tensor, qscheme: QScheme, qdtype: QDtype, ch_axis: int | None = None)

members.summary: Immutable bundle of the parameters that define a quantized tensor.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description:
Quantization step; scalar for per-tensor, length-`C` for
per-channel.

parameters.name: zero_point
parameters.description: Integer offset (stored as an integer-valued float tensor).

parameters.name: ch_axis
parameters.description: Channel axis for per-channel schemes; `None` for per-tensor.

members.name: calculate_qparams
members.signature: calculate_qparams(min_val: _RangeLike, max_val: _RangeLike, qscheme: QScheme, qdtype: QDtype, eps: float = 1e-08)
members.summary: Derive `(scale, zero_point)` from an observed value range.
members.extended:
Implements the standard affine / symmetric calibration.  The observed
range is first extended to include zero (so real 0 is always
representable), then:

* **affine** — $s = (\max - \min)/(q_\max - q_\min)$,
  $z = \operatorname{clip}(q_\min - \operatorname{round}(\min/s), q_\min, q_\max)$;
* **symmetric** — $s = \max(|\min|, |\max|) / ((q_\max - q_\min)/2)$ with `z` pinned to 0 (signed) or mid-range
  (unsigned).

Works elementwise, so scalar `min_val` / `max_val` yield per-tensor
params and length-`C` inputs yield per-channel params.

parameters.name: min_val
parameters.description:
Observed minimum / maximum (per-tensor scalars or per-channel
vectors).

parameters.name: max_val
parameters.description:
Observed minimum / maximum (per-tensor scalars or per-channel
vectors).

parameters.name: eps
parameters.description:
Lower floor on `scale` to avoid division by zero on a degenerate
(constant) range.

members.name: QDtype
members.signature: QDtype(name: str, bits: int, signed: bool, quant_min: int, quant_max: int, storage: str)

methods.name: __repr__
methods.summary: Return `"lucid.quantization.<name>"`.

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(name: str, bits: int, signed: bool, quant_min: int, quant_max: int, storage: str)

members.summary: Descriptor for a quantized integer grid.
members.extended:
Unlike the reference framework, Lucid does not add quantized dtypes
to the C++ engine enum; a `QDtype` is a pure-Python descriptor
and the integer codes live in an ordinary `storage` dtype.  This
keeps the whole quantization subsystem off the engine's critical
path until a real low-precision GEMM is wired in.

parameters.name: storage
parameters.description:
Name of the Lucid dtype that physically holds the codes.  `quint8`
maps to `int16` for now (the engine has no `uint8`); `qint4`
maps to `int8` (unpacked) until 4-bit packing lands.

members.name: QScheme
members.signature: QScheme()

methods.name: is_per_channel
methods.summary: Return `True` for the two per-channel schemes.

methods.name: is_symmetric
methods.summary: Return `True` when `zero_point` is pinned (symmetric schemes).

members.summary: How `scale` / `zero_point` map real values onto the integer grid.
notes:
PER_TENSOR_AFFINE
    One `(scale, zero_point)` for the whole tensor; `zero_point`
    is free, so asymmetric ranges (e.g. post-ReLU activations) map
    without wasting codes.
PER_TENSOR_SYMMETRIC
    One `scale` for the whole tensor with `zero_point` pinned
    (0 for signed, mid-range for unsigned); real 0 maps exactly to
    an integer code.
PER_CHANNEL_AFFINE
    One `(scale, zero_point)` per channel along a chosen axis.
PER_CHANNEL_SYMMETRIC
    One `scale` per channel with pinned `zero_point` — the usual
    choice for convolution / linear weights.

members.name: FakeQuantize
members.signature: FakeQuantize(observer: type[ObserverBase] = MovingAverageMinMaxObserver, observer_kwargs: object = {})

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Refresh qparams (if observing) then fake-quantize (if enabled).

methods.name: enable_observer
methods.summary: Enable/disable statistic collection; returns `self` for chaining.

methods.name: disable_observer
methods.summary: Freeze the observer (stop refreshing `scale` / `zero_point`).

methods.name: enable_fake_quant
methods.summary: Enable/disable the fake-quant transform; returns `self`.

methods.name: disable_fake_quant
methods.summary: Pass activations through unchanged (observer may still run).

methods.name: calculate_qparams
methods.summary: Delegate to the wrapped observer.

methods.name: with_args
methods.summary: Return a zero-arg factory building this module with `kwargs`.

members.summary: Observer + straight-through fake-quantization, toggleable for QAT.

parameters.name: observer
parameters.description: Observer class instantiated to track statistics.

members.name: FixedQParamsObserver
members.signature: FixedQParamsObserver(scale: float, zero_point: int, qscheme: QScheme = per_tensor_affine, qdtype: QDtype = quint8, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(scale: float, zero_point: int, qscheme: QScheme = per_tensor_affine, qdtype: QDtype = quint8, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Identity — the qparams are fixed, so no statistics are gathered.

methods.name: calculate_qparams
methods.summary: Return `(scale, zero_point)` from the accumulated statistics.

members.summary: Observer with a fixed `(scale, zero_point)` — no statistics collected.
members.extended:
For ops whose output range is known a priori (`sigmoid` → `[0, 1]`,
`tanh` → `[-1, 1]`, `softmax` → `[0, 1]`): the grid is fixed, so
calibration would be wasted.  `forward` is the identity and
`calculate_qparams` returns the constructor-supplied qparams verbatim.

parameters.name: zero_point
parameters.description: The fixed integer mapped to real value `0`.

parameters.name: qscheme
parameters.description: Target quantization scheme (metadata only; qparams are fixed).

parameters.name: eps
parameters.description: Retained for interface symmetry; unused (the `scale` is fixed).

members.name: HistogramObserver
members.signature: HistogramObserver(bins: int = 2048, qscheme: QScheme = per_tensor_affine, qdtype: QDtype = quint8, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(bins: int = 2048, qscheme: QScheme = per_tensor_affine, qdtype: QDtype = quint8, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Update the running range and accumulate the histogram.

methods.name: calculate_qparams
methods.summary: Return `(scale, zero_point)` from the accumulated statistics.

members.summary: Per-tensor observer that records a value histogram and picks a clip range.
members.extended:
Accumulates a `bins`-bucket histogram (via the engine `histc`
primitive) over the running `[min, max]`, rebinning when the range
grows.  `calculate_qparams` then selects the `[new_min, new_max]`
clip that minimises the expected L2 quantization error estimated from
the histogram — trading a little clipping for much finer resolution on
heavy-tailed activations.

members.name: MinMaxObserver
members.signature: MinMaxObserver(qscheme: QScheme = per_tensor_affine, qdtype: QDtype = quint8, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(qscheme: QScheme = per_tensor_affine, qdtype: QDtype = quint8, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Fold `x`'s global min/max into the running statistics.

methods.name: calculate_qparams
methods.summary: Delegate to the wrapped observer.

members.summary: Per-tensor observer tracking the global running min / max of the input.
members.extended:
The default activation / weight statistic: every batch's overall `min` /
`max` is folded into a running range, from which `calculate_qparams`
derives a single per-tensor `(scale, zero_point)`.  Simple and robust,
though sensitive to outliers — see `MovingAverageMinMaxObserver` and
`HistogramObserver` for outlier-tolerant variants.

parameters.name: eps
parameters.description:
Lower floor on the derived `scale` (avoids division by zero on a
constant input).

members.name: MovingAverageMinMaxObserver
members.signature: MovingAverageMinMaxObserver(qscheme: QScheme = per_tensor_affine, qdtype: QDtype = quint8, averaging_constant: float = 0.01, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(qscheme: QScheme = per_tensor_affine, qdtype: QDtype = quint8, averaging_constant: float = 0.01, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: EMA-update the running min/max (seeded on the first batch).

members.summary: Per-tensor observer with an exponential moving average of min / max.
members.extended:
Smooths the running range across calibration batches, which is more
robust to per-batch outliers than a hard running min/max.

members.name: MovingAveragePerChannelMinMaxObserver
members.signature: MovingAveragePerChannelMinMaxObserver(ch_axis: int = 0, qscheme: QScheme = per_channel_symmetric, qdtype: QDtype = qint8, averaging_constant: float = 0.01, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(ch_axis: int = 0, qscheme: QScheme = per_channel_symmetric, qdtype: QDtype = qint8, averaging_constant: float = 0.01, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: EMA-update the per-channel min/max (seeded on the first batch).

members.summary: Per-channel observer with an EMA of the per-channel min / max.
members.extended:
The per-channel analogue of `MovingAverageMinMaxObserver` — smooths
each channel's range across calibration batches (robust to per-batch
outliers), which the reference framework ships as a distinct observer.

parameters.name: averaging_constant
parameters.description: EMA weight of each new batch's range; smaller is smoother / slower.

members.name: NoopObserver
members.signature: NoopObserver(qscheme: QScheme = per_tensor_affine, qdtype: QDtype = quint8, eps: float = 1e-08)
members.summary: A no-op `PlaceholderObserver` — pure identity, collects no stats.
members.extended:
Named alias the reference framework uses to mark tensors that should pass
through the observed graph **without** any statistic gathering (e.g. an
already-quantized input, or a layer deliberately excluded from calibration).
Inherits the `qscheme` / `qdtype` / `eps` constructor of
`PlaceholderObserver` unchanged.

members.name: ObserverBase
members.signature: ObserverBase(qscheme: QScheme, qdtype: QDtype, ch_axis: int | None = None, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(qscheme: QScheme, qdtype: QDtype, ch_axis: int | None = None, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: calculate_qparams
methods.summary: Return `(scale, zero_point)` from the accumulated statistics.

methods.name: with_args
methods.summary: Return a zero-arg factory that builds this observer with `kwargs`.
methods.extended:
Mirrors the reference framework's `Observer.with_args` ergonomic so
a `lucid.quantization.QConfig` can carry an observer *recipe*
rather than a live instance.

members.summary: Base class for statistics collectors that yield `(scale, zero_point)`.

parameters.name: ch_axis
parameters.description: Channel axis; forced to `None` for per-tensor schemes.

members.name: PerChannelMinMaxObserver
members.signature: PerChannelMinMaxObserver(ch_axis: int = 0, qscheme: QScheme = per_channel_symmetric, qdtype: QDtype = qint8, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(ch_axis: int = 0, qscheme: QScheme = per_channel_symmetric, qdtype: QDtype = qint8, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Fold per-channel min/max (reduced over all non-channel axes).

methods.name: calculate_qparams
methods.summary: Return `(scale, zero_point)` from the accumulated statistics.

members.summary: Per-channel observer tracking a running min / max along `ch_axis`.
members.extended:
The standard **weight** observer: it keeps an independent `[min, max]` per
channel (reducing over every other axis), so `calculate_qparams`
returns a *vector* of `(scale, zero_point)` — one per output channel.
Per-channel granularity is markedly more accurate than per-tensor for conv /
linear kernels whose channels differ in dynamic range.

parameters.name: ch_axis
parameters.description:
Axis whose entries are quantized independently (output channels — axis 0
for conv / linear weights).

members.name: PlaceholderObserver
members.signature: PlaceholderObserver(qscheme: QScheme = per_tensor_affine, qdtype: QDtype = quint8, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(qscheme: QScheme = per_tensor_affine, qdtype: QDtype = quint8, eps: float = 1e-08)

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Identity — no statistics are gathered.

methods.name: calculate_qparams
methods.summary: Return `(scale, zero_point)` from the accumulated statistics.

members.summary: Carries `qdtype` metadata but collects no statistics.
members.extended:
Used where activation qparams are produced at *runtime* (dynamic quant) or
deliberately absent — the observer only records the target dtype / scheme,
so `calculate_qparams` raises rather than returning a range.

parameters.name: qscheme
parameters.description: Target quantization scheme recorded for downstream consumers.

parameters.name: qdtype
parameters.description: Target quantized dtype recorded for downstream consumers.

parameters.name: eps
parameters.description: Retained for interface symmetry; unused (no range is derived).

members.name: QConfig
members.signature: QConfig()
members.summary: A pair of observer / fake-quant factories for activations and weights.
members.extended:
The atomic quantization recipe: `prepare` / `prepare_qat` call the two
factories to build a fresh observer (PTQ) or `FakeQuantize` (QAT) at
every insertion site.  A `QConfigMapping` then decides *which*
QConfig applies to *which* module.  Both fields are zero-arg callables —
typically produced by `Observer.with_args(...)` /
`FakeQuantize.with_args(...)` — so one recipe instantiates independent
modules across the whole network.

members.name: QConfigMapping
members.signature: QConfigMapping()

methods.name: set_global
methods.summary: Set the fallback QConfig applied when nothing more specific matches.

methods.name: set_object_type
methods.summary: Bind a QConfig to every module of `module_type`.

methods.name: set_module_name
methods.summary: Bind a QConfig to the module at the qualified `name`.

methods.name: get_qconfig
methods.summary: Return the QConfig for a module, most-specific match first.

members.summary: Resolves which `QConfig` applies to each module.
members.extended:
Resolution order (most specific wins): qualified **module name** →
**module type** → **global**.  Setters return `self` for fluent
chaining, mirroring the reference framework.

members.name: get_default_qat_qconfig
members.signature: get_default_qat_qconfig()
members.summary: Default **QAT** config — the same schemes wrapped in `FakeQuantize`.

members.name: get_default_qat_qconfig_mapping
members.signature: get_default_qat_qconfig_mapping()
members.summary: Build the default **QAT** `QConfigMapping`.
members.extended:
Sets `get_default_qat_qconfig` as the global fallback, so every
eligible module trains with `FakeQuantize` on its activations and
weights unless overridden by type or name.

returns.description: A mapping whose global QConfig is the default QAT config.

members.name: get_default_qconfig
members.signature: get_default_qconfig()
members.summary: Default **static PTQ** config.
members.extended:
Activations use a per-tensor-affine `quint8` histogram observer
(high accuracy on heavy-tailed activations); weights use a
per-channel-symmetric `qint8` min/max observer — the standard,
accuracy-preserving combination for convolution / linear layers.

members.name: get_default_qconfig_mapping
members.signature: get_default_qconfig_mapping()
members.summary: Build the default **static-PTQ** `QConfigMapping`.
members.extended:
Sets `get_default_qconfig` as the global fallback, so every eligible
module is quantized with the standard histogram-activation /
per-channel-weight recipe unless overridden by type or name.

returns.description: A mapping whose global QConfig is the default PTQ config.

members.name: convert
members.signature: convert(model: nn.Module, inplace: bool = False)
members.summary: Swap calibrated float modules for their quantized replacements.
members.extended:
When `lucid.backends.quantized.use_mlx()` is true (the MLX quantized
engine is present), Linear-family layers are routed to the real weight-only
`quantized_matmul` GEMM — the genuine speed + memory win — instead of the
dequantize-to-float path.  Set `backends.quantized.engine = "reference"`
to force the exact W8A8 dequant numerics on every layer.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description:
A model previously returned by `prepare` and run through
calibration, so every quantizable submodule carries frozen activation
qparams.

parameters.name: inplace
parameters.description: Mutate `model` in place instead of deep-copying it before conversion.

returns.description:
The converted, evaluation-mode model with int8 weights baked in and its
float submodules replaced by their quantized counterparts.

members.name: prepare
members.signature: prepare(model: nn.Module, qconfig: QConfig | QConfigMapping | None = None, inplace: bool = False)
members.summary: Insert activation observers ahead of calibration.

parameters.name: inplace
parameters.description: Mutate `model` in place instead of deep-copying it.

returns.description:
The prepared model — run calibration data through it, then
`convert`.

members.name: prepare_qat
members.signature: prepare_qat(model: nn.Module, qconfig: QConfig | QConfigMapping | None = None, inplace: bool = False)
members.summary: Insert fake-quant modules for quantization-aware training.
members.extended:
Swaps weighted layers for their `lucid.nn.qat` counterparts (weight +
output fake-quant) and attaches a fake-quant to every `QuantStub`.  The
returned model is left in `train` mode — fine-tune it, then
`convert` to the int8 inference model.

parameters.name: inplace
parameters.description: Mutate in place instead of deep-copying.

members.name: quantize_dynamic
members.signature: quantize_dynamic(model: nn.Module, qconfig_spec: set[type] | None = None, dtype: QDtype = qint8, inplace: bool = False)
members.summary: Dynamically quantize a model — int8 weights, runtime-quantized activations.
members.extended:
Unlike static PTQ this needs **no calibration**: every module whose type
is in `qconfig_spec` (default `{Linear, LSTM}`) is swapped for its
dynamic counterpart, which measures the activation range per forward.

parameters.name: qconfig_spec
parameters.description: Which module types to quantize; defaults to `{nn.Linear, nn.LSTM}`.

parameters.name: inplace
parameters.description: Mutate in place instead of deep-copying.

members.name: convert_fx
members.signature: convert_fx(model: nn.Module, inplace: bool = False)
members.summary: Graph-mode convert — bake a calibrated model into quantized modules.
members.extended:
The graph-mode counterpart of `lucid.quantization.convert`: it swaps
each observed float module for its quantized inference equivalent, folding in
the calibrated qparams.  The result is a plain quantized model, ready to run
or hand to `lucid.compile`.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: A model returned by `prepare_fx` and then calibrated.

parameters.name: inplace
parameters.description: Mutate `model` in place instead of deep-copying it first.

members.name: prepare_fx
members.signature: prepare_fx(model: nn.Module, qconfig_mapping: QConfig | QConfigMapping | None = None, example_inputs: Tensor | None = None, inplace: bool = False)
members.summary: Graph-mode prepare — auto-wrap quant boundaries and insert observers.

parameters.name: qconfig_mapping
parameters.description: Quantization recipe; defaults to the static-PTQ default.

parameters.name: example_inputs
parameters.description: Reserved for future trace-time insertion; unused on the composite path.

parameters.name: inplace
parameters.description: Mutate in place instead of deep-copying.

members.name: fuse_modules
members.signature: fuse_modules(model: nn.Module, modules_to_fuse: Sequence[Sequence[str]], inplace: bool = False)
members.summary: Fuse the given groups of modules for quantization.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Model to fuse (put in `eval` mode — BN folding is eval-time).

parameters.name: modules_to_fuse
parameters.description:
Each group is a list of dotted module names to fuse, e.g.
`[["conv1", "bn1"], ["layer1.0.conv1", "layer1.0.bn1"]]`.  Supported
runs: `[Conv, BN]`, `[Conv, BN, ReLU]`, `[Conv, ReLU]`,
`[Linear, ReLU]`.

parameters.name: inplace
parameters.description: Fuse in place instead of on a deep copy.

members.name: fuse_modules_qat
members.signature: fuse_modules_qat(model: nn.Module, modules_to_fuse: Sequence[Sequence[str]], qconfig: QConfig, inplace: bool = False)
members.summary: Fuse for **QAT** — Conv+BN runs become trainable `nn.intrinsic.qat` modules.
members.extended:
Unlike `fuse_modules` (which eval-folds BN into the conv weight),
a `[Conv, BN]` / `[Conv, BN, ReLU]` run becomes a trainable
`ConvBn*` / `ConvBnReLU*` that folds BN **per forward** so the BN
parameters keep training under the straight-through estimator.  `[Conv,
ReLU]` / `[Linear, ReLU]` (no BN) become the plain intrinsic fused
module, which `prepare_qat` then swaps to its QAT form.

parameters.name: qconfig
parameters.description:
QAT recipe supplied to the fused `ConvBn*` modules (they fake-quant
at construction, so the qconfig is needed here rather than later).

parameters.name: inplace
parameters.description: Fuse in place instead of on a deep copy.

summary: `lucid.quantization` — post-training quantization, QAT, and quantized inference.
extended:
This sub-package brings weights and activations from `float32` down to
low-precision integer grids (int8 / int4), the top capability lever for
inference and serving on Apple Silicon.  It mirrors the reference
framework's quantization surface while keeping a single canonical path
per name (H8): the *workflow* API lives here (observers, `QConfig`,
`prepare` / `convert` / `quantize_dynamic` / `prepare_qat`), and
the quantized *module* layer lives under `lucid.nn.quantized` /
`lucid.nn.qat` / `lucid.nn.intrinsic`.

Representation follows the *sidecar* design (B): a quantized weight is an
ordinary integer `lucid.Tensor` plus float `scale` /
`zero_point` buffers held by the owning module — no dedicated quantized
tensor subtype and no new engine dtype.

**Phase 0 surface (this commit):** the schemes / dtypes / parameter
container and the three arithmetic primitives (`quantize`,
`dequantize`, `fake_quantize`).  Observers, `QConfig`, the
eager / dynamic / graph workflows, and the real low-precision GEMM land
in later phases.
